nodes.description: The main application or tool configured by this file, responsible for generating project wikis using LLMs.
The specific Large Language Model (LLM) to be used by llmwikidoc for generating content.
The directory where the generated wiki documentation will be stored.
The depth of context to consider when processing information for wiki generation.
A group of settings that define how confidence scores are managed and updated within llmwikidoc.
The starting confidence score assigned to new information or statements.
The amount by which a confidence score increases when information is reinforced.
The amount by which a confidence score decreases when information is contradicted.
The number of days over which confidence scores decay for information.
A group of settings that define the relative importance of different search algorithms used by llmwikidoc.
The weighting factor for the BM25 search algorithm in the overall search relevance calculation.
The weighting factor for vector-based search in the overall search relevance calculation.
The weighting factor for graph-based search in the overall search relevance calculation.
The core product, a general agentic system based on distributed skills.
nodes.id: Sistema Agéntica General basado en Skills Distribuidos

nodes.description: Project Requirements Document, serving as a high-confidence source for intended functionality.
The core framework (`google-genai`) upon which the agentic platform is built, enabling tool-calling and skill injection.
GitHub acting as the Single Source of Truth for the distributed skill catalog.
A JSON Schema standard used for defining skill contracts (skill.json).
Part of the core technological stack, likely for API development and runtime.
A secure execution environment for TypeScript skills, invoked as a subprocess with strict permissions and a hard timeout.
A secure execution channel for dynamic code and mathematical computations using Extism/Wasmtime.
Traditional agentic solutions that suffer from architectural rigidity, defining the problem statement.
The product solution: a decoupled and dynamically extensible agentic platform.
A YAML configuration file for discovering and managing skills.
A mechanism for secure, lightweight, and audited runtime injection of skills.
A directory structure for each skill, containing its definition and documentation.
A file defining an external skill, likely adhering to the Anthropic Tool Definition Schema, including an input_schema.
Markdown content describing a skill, fetched from GitHub and injected into the agent context.
The central component responsible for orchestrating the overall agentic workflow, including skill discovery and execution.
A sub-agent responsible for dynamically discovering and loading skills from the GitHub catalog.
A local indexed cache used by the orchestrator for semantic matching of skills.
A Directed Acyclic Graph representing the dynamic chaining of multi-skill sequential workflows.
The execution engine that processes LLM-validated payloads through regulated channels.
An execution channel mandatory for closed dynamic code and mathematical utility calculations, running Extism/Wasmtime.
A secure execution channel for TypeScript/JavaScript scripts with granular network permissions and timeouts.
A native bidirectional integration channel for corporate relational and vector databases using secure RPC.
A future execution channel prepared for isolated ephemeral container execution in Oracle OKE.
An example TypeScript skill mentioned for end-to-end validation.
An example TypeScript skill mentioned for end-to-end validation.
A skill for specifying test cases based on the SCRUM methodology.
A skill to evaluate the reliability of a software system.
A skill for performing basic mathematical operations.
A skill for managing a vector knowledge base in Qdrant (mykb) for storage and semantic search.
A skill for managing groups, users, and issues in GitLab.
Runtime environment used by the WebAssembly Sandbox for code execution.
Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes, targeted for future Docker channel deployments.
The project name for a CLI-based agentic platform with dynamic skill discovery and execution.
A GitHub-hosted collection of skills, indexed by `catalog.yaml` and containing individual `skill.json` definitions.
The command-line interface entry point for the application.
The JSON Schema standard that `skill.json` files must conform to, defining the contract for LLM-populated parameters.
An existing component responsible for fetching the catalog index, filtering by tags, and lazy-loading skill definitions.
A new component responsible for converting SkillDefinition models into ADK FunctionTool instances and validating parameters.
An ADK class instance representing a callable tool derived from a skill, injected dynamically into the agent.
A Pydantic model representing the full definition and contract of a skill (from skill.json).
A Pydantic model defining the JSON schema for skill input parameters, used for validation.
Coordinating Agent routes user prompt via confidence-gated fallback — if confidence < configurable threshold (default 0.72), delegates to CatalogExplorer.
Pass 1 tag extraction constrains output to the catalog's actual tag vocabulary to prevent open-vocabulary mismatch.
CatalogExplorer fetches `catalog.yaml` from GitHub SSOT and filters skills by tag intersection (already built — this requirement covers contract verification against new SkillInjector).
Matched skill's `skill.json` is lazy-loaded from GitHub SSOT per request (already built — this requirement covers integration into the v1 pipeline).
SkillInjector converts `SkillDefinition` (from `skill.json`) to a live ADK `FunctionTool` via `BaseToolset.get_tools()` for runtime injection into the agent's active tool context.

LLM payload is validated against `skill.json` `input_schema` (JSON Schema) before Deno fires — missing required fields block the call and return a structured correction request to the agent (no infrastructure call made).
`SKILL.md` cognitive guide content is fetched from GitHub and injected into agent context alongside `skill.json`.

Matched skill executes via Deno subprocess with `--allow-net=<validated-domain>` (domain validated against regex before flag construction), no file I/O permissions, hard 5000ms timeout; process group killed on timeout with no zombie processes.
Execution errors return typed structured results (timeout / validation_failure / execution_error) — not generic Python exceptions propagated raw to the user.
CLI shows a progress indicator during the Deno execution window so the user knows the skill is running.
`catalog.yaml` responses are TTL-cached in-memory (5-minute TTL) to prevent GitHub rate limit exhaustion during active development.
`GITHUB_TOKEN` env var supported for authenticated GitHub fetches (5000 req/hr vs 60 unauthenticated).
Confidence threshold is externalized to config (env var or config file) — re-calibratable without code change.
Each routing decision logged to JSONL (prompt hash, extracted tags, confidence score, routing decision, matched skill name if any).
User runs `python main.py` from terminal, enters a natural-language prompt, receives a result.
End-to-end happy path verified with at least one real TypeScript skill from the GitHub catalog (`evaluar-test-case` or `especificar_user_story`).
Multi-skill DAG chaining — single prompt triggers sequential execution of multiple skills (user story → test case → evaluation) (v2).
WebAssembly/Extism channel for closed-compute skills (calculator) — microsecond latency, no network, fully isolated memory (v2).
ISO 27001 / BASC-aligned immutable execution logs — full lifecycle capture from prompt to output (v2).
A configuration parameter related to the operational mode, set to 'yolo'.
A configuration parameter related to processing depth, set to 'standard'.
A boolean configuration parameter enabling or disabling parallel execution.
A boolean configuration parameter indicating whether documentation commits are enabled.
A configuration parameter defining the model's performance profile, set to 'balanced'.
A nested configuration object defining various workflow stages (research, plan_check, verifier) and their enablement status.
The recommended runtime for the project, chosen for stability and asyncio.TaskGroup support.
The platform hosting the skill catalog and individual skill definitions.
A Pydantic model representing the index structure of the skill catalog.
A Pydantic model representing a single skill's entry in the catalog index.
The Python client library for interacting with the Gemini API.
A decision to defer adding an execution channel based on WebAssembly and Extism until v2, focusing on the Deno channel for v1.
A planned channel for Qdrant knowledge base integration (v2).
A future execution channel for SRE-class skills (out of scope for v1).
A decision to defer complex chaining of multiple skills into directed acyclic graphs until v2, prioritizing single-skill loop validation in v1.
The initial phase of a project focused on creating a secure and isolated execution channel for Deno-based skills.
A new component that acts as an `asyncio.create_subprocess_exec` wrapper for executing Deno processes, handling timeouts and error propagation.
The second milestone, converting skill definitions into callable ADK FunctionTools.
Google ADK's mechanism for exposing Python callables to the LLM as tools, used by SkillInjector to wrap Deno-based skills.
A mechanism for validating LLM-supplied parameters against a defined schema.
The third milestone, establishing the agent's routing logic based on confidence and tags.
A new orchestration component that implements the two-pass routing logic, manages confidence thresholds, and wires all sub-components.
A routing strategy where the agent's confidence score dictates whether to answer directly or route to an external catalog.
The process of identifying and extracting relevant tags from a user prompt.
The practice of logging routing decisions as JSON Line records.
The fourth milestone, integrating with the GitHub-hosted skill catalog with in-memory caching and rate-limit protection.
A time-to-live cache used to store catalog data from GitHub to reduce network calls.
An environment variable used for authenticated requests to GitHub, providing higher API rate limits.
Mechanisms to avoid exceeding GitHub API request limits.
The fifth and final milestone, providing a user-facing command-line interface for end-to-end interaction.
A command-line interface component enabling interactive prompt sessions, progress indicators, and user-readable error messages.
User input provided to the agent in an unstructured, human-readable format.
A skill implementation written in TypeScript, intended for execution by DenoRunner.
A JavaScript/TypeScript runtime used for executing skills in a secure sandbox.
Large Language Model, which supplies parameters to ADK FunctionTools.
A project overview document, serving as a high-confidence source for project intentions.
The guiding principle of the project: dynamic skill discovery and execution from any prompt.
The architecturally mandated sequence of phases for project delivery.
The decision to use 'taskkill /F /T /PID' for subprocess cleanup on Windows 11 in Phase 1.
The decision to always use 'proc.communicate()' over 'proc.wait()' in Phase 1 to avoid pipe deadlock.
A Windows command used for forcefully terminating processes by Process ID (PID).
A Python function for killing a process group, noted as unavailable on Windows 11 for this project.
A Python subprocess method recommended for interacting with process I/O to avoid pipe deadlocks.
A Python subprocess method for waiting for process termination, generally avoided in this project due to potential deadlocks.
A specific JSON output design required for confidence score extraction in Phase 3.
An area of low confidence regarding Deno's handling of network redirects, requiring verification.
A specific version of the Deno runtime against which network redirect behavior will be verified.
A generic term referring to the intelligent component that performs discovery, injection, validation, and execution of skills.
A locked decision outlining the communication contract between Python and Deno skills, including JSON over stdin/stdout and stderr usage.
A locked decision specifying how Deno permissions, particularly `--allow-net` domains, are managed and validated before subprocess creation.
A locked decision defining the structured Pydantic models used for all execution outcomes (success, timeout, execution error, validation failure).
A Pydantic model representing a successful skill execution result, containing parsed JSON data from stdout.
A Pydantic model representing a skill execution timeout, including the elapsed time in milliseconds.
A Pydantic model representing a skill execution failure, including the exit code and stderr output.
A Pydantic model representing an error due to invalid input, specifically an invalid domain string provided for `--allow-net`.
A Python library used for data validation and settings management, adopted for modeling the typed results of DenoRunner execution.
Deno's standard input stream, used by DenoRunner to pass JSON parameters to skills and read by skills for input.
Deno's standard output stream, used by skills to return a single JSON result and read by DenoRunner for output.
Deno's standard error stream, exclusively reserved for error output from skill execution.
The primary method of DenoRunner that spawns a Deno subprocess, passes parameters, enforces timeouts, handles process cleanup, and returns structured Pydantic results.
The Python module containing the `DenoRunner` class implementation.
The test suite for the `DenoRunner` class.
A minimal TypeScript skill used as a test fixture, designed to read JSON from stdin and echo it to stdout.
A popular test runner for Python applications.
A pytest plugin for writing asynchronous tests.
An area of implementation detail left to the discretion of the developer (Claude), concerning how to terminate Deno subprocesses effectively.
An architectural decision point left to the discretion of the developer, regarding the choice of concurrency model for subprocess execution.
An area left to the discretion of the developer, concerning how to identify and prevent zombie processes and ensure proper cleanup in tests.
An implementation detail concerning the specific hierarchy of Pydantic models for results, left to the discretion of the developer.
An acceptance criterion related to the enforcement of execution timeouts.
External files containing business logic written in TypeScript, executed by the DenoRunner via Deno.
The predefined file and directory layout for the `DenoRunner` and associated components like result models and tests.
An architectural pattern for managing Python `asyncio` subprocesses, including creation, input/output handling, timeout enforcement, and robust process tree cleanup, especially on Windows.
nodes.id: asyncio Subprocess with Timeout and Cleanup

nodes.description: The Python module containing the Pydantic result type models (`ExecutionSuccess`, `TimeoutError`, `ExecutionError`, `ValidationFailure`).
An asyncio function for creating a new subprocess and executing a command.
An asyncio function for running a coroutine with a timeout.
The overarching system design allowing agents to dynamically discover and execute skills.
A core mechanism where the agent's confidence in directly executing a task determines whether it triggers skill discovery and injection.
Skills (tools) are not loaded upfront but are discovered and injected into the agent's context only when needed, reducing initial overhead.
Execution of skills in isolated Deno subprocesses with controlled permissions and timeouts for security and stability.
The CLI entry point, responsible for parsing user arguments, starting the event loop, and initiating the agent's run.
The underlying Google ADK agent component that CoordinatingAgent wraps and delegates LLM interactions to.
The Large Language Model used by the CoordinatingAgent for prompt processing, confidence evaluation, and tag extraction.
The external JavaScript/TypeScript runtime used for executing sandboxed skills as subprocesses.
A group of models (CatalogManifest, CatalogSkill, SkillDefinition, InputSchema) that define the data contracts for skills and catalog entries, residing in `src/models/skill.py`.
Python's built-in library for writing concurrent code using the async/await syntax.
An architectural pattern where the agent performs an initial LLM pass for confidence and tag extraction, and a second pass with injected tools if confidence is low.
An architectural pattern where SkillInjector wraps the Deno execution logic within an ADK FunctionTool, using its signature for LLM interaction and parameter validation.
An architectural pattern for skill execution using non-blocking asynchronous subprocesses (Deno) with strict time limits.
The primary domain of the project, focusing on an agent system controlled via a command-line interface.
The mechanism by which the agent finds and integrates new capabilities (skills) from a dynamic marketplace.
A system where tools (skills) can be discovered and used on the fly.
The ability of the agent to map user prompts in natural language to the appropriate skill.
A method to efficiently identify relevant skills using a lightweight catalog index and pre-filtering based on tags.
The mechanism to load the full contract of a skill (from its skill.json file) only when it has been matched by pre-filtering.
The critical missing piece and primary build target for v1, enabling runtime skill loading into the agent.
Validation of LLM-generated skill parameters against a defined JSON schema before skill execution.
The strategy for running TypeScript skills in an isolated and permission-controlled environment using Deno.
A mechanism to terminate skill execution if it exceeds a predefined time limit.
Handling and returning controlled, distinct error states for different types of execution failures (validation, runtime, timeout).
The main command-line interface for interacting with and running the agent.
The foundational acceptance criterion for the initial version, ensuring a complete cycle of discovery, injection, validation, and execution for a single skill.
Allows new skills to be made available to the agent simply by updating a catalog, without requiring agent code changes or redeployment.
Optimizes skill discovery by using cached or local knowledge when an LLM is highly confident, and falling back to a live catalog search when uncertain.
A method to reduce LLM token consumption by programmatically filtering skills based on tag intersections before involving the LLM.
An architectural choice using a lightweight root index (catalog.yaml) and separate full skill contracts (skill.json) to scale efficiently.
A human-readable Markdown document accompanying each skill to provide business rules, examples, and constraints for LLM guidance.
The ability to automatically route different categories of skills (e.g., compute, I/O) to appropriate, specialized sandbox environments.
nodes.id: Execution channel routing by skill type

nodes.description: A comprehensive, unalterable record of every step of an agent's request lifecycle, from prompt to execution output, for compliance and analysis.
A decision to defer development of a graphical web interface or FastAPI endpoint until v2, focusing on CLI for v1 validation.
A decision to defer automatic caching of skill lookups using vector databases due to v1 scope and current performance expectations.
A decision to defer features for multiple users, permissions, and billing until v2, focusing on a personal-use CLI in v1.
A decision to defer real-time streaming of LLM responses until v2, prioritizing synchronous generation for v1 CLI.
A decision to defer formal skill versioning and rollback mechanisms until v2, using Git tags for version control in v1.
A decision to defer an automated CI/CD pipeline for publishing skills until v2, relying on manual GitHub PRs for v1.
Google's Agent Development Kit, serving as the framework for the Gemini agent.
A reference for defining tool schemas, influencing the project's design.
Design patterns for secure and isolated execution environments using Deno.
A Python library used for validating JSON data against a JSON schema.
A Python function used to run external processes, specifically for Deno sandbox execution with timeout enforcement.
A specific requirement from the PRD concerning JSON Schema parameter validation.
A specific requirement from the PRD concerning the immutable audit log.
An international standard for information security management, relevant for the immutable audit log.
Business Alliance for Secure Commerce, a standard relevant for the immutable audit log's compliance.
A Python web framework considered for future web UI development.
A vector database considered for future vector caching capabilities.
A WebAssembly host runtime, considered for an alternative execution channel.
A binary instruction format for a stack-based virtual machine, considered for an alternative execution channel.
A platform or environment mentioned as a potential execution channel for vector DB skills.
A feature of Git used as a simple versioning mechanism for the skills catalog in v1.
A numeric value used in intent routing to determine whether to trust local knowledge or query the live catalog.
The target domain for the project, emphasizing distributed skill discovery and Deno sandbox execution.
The process of providing an LLM (specifically Gemini) with definitions of available tools (skills) and their schemas.
The mechanism for running Deno skills in isolated subprocesses to prevent security issues and manage resources.
The fixed token budget of Gemini models that is shared among system prompts, tool schemas, conversation history, and user prompts.
Agent Development Kit, a framework that handles skill injection and execution within the agentic platform.
A standard for describing the structure and validation rules for JSON data, used for skill parameter validation.
An external service used by the CatalogExplorer to fetch skill metadata and content, subject to rate limits.
A utility function to measure the token count of a payload, specifically for Gemini model inputs, to prevent context window overflow.
A technique using `os.killpg` on POSIX systems to terminate a parent process and all its child processes simultaneously, preventing zombies.
A Windows-specific mechanism to group processes together, allowing for reliable termination of an entire process tree.
The practice of explicitly specifying a fixed LLM model version (e.g., `gemini-2.0-flash-001`) to ensure consistent behavior and enable threshold re-calibration upon updates.
A testing and calibration strategy where both cached and catalog-fetched paths are executed without live traffic to compare results and tune parameters like confidence thresholds.
The `raw.githubusercontent.com` endpoint, which is CDN-backed and has higher rate limits than the main GitHub API, suitable for fetching raw file content.
A caching mechanism for `catalog.yaml` and `skill.json` files to reduce repeated requests to GitHub, implementing a TTL for the catalog.
An optional authentication token that can be provided as an environment variable (`GITHUB_TOKEN`) to raise GitHub API rate limits significantly.
The practice of using libraries like `pytest-mock` and `responses` to simulate HTTP requests in unit and integration tests, avoiding live external service calls.
A JSON Schema keyword that, when set to `false`, prevents the validation from silently passing payloads with unexpected extra fields.
A development practice involving automated checks in CI to ensure `skill.json` files conform to best practices, such as explicitly including `additionalProperties: false`.
An overview of the technology investigation for the project, including domain, research date, and confidence.
The proposed set of core technologies, supporting libraries, and development tools.
The primary software components essential for the project's runtime and core functionality.
Auxiliary libraries that provide specific functionalities, often as transitive dependencies.
Utilities and software used for local development, testing, and pre-execution validation.
Recommended architectural patterns and code examples for using the google-adk library.
The canonical agent class from google-adk, used for orchestrating AI agent behavior and tool invocation.
A google-adk component responsible for managing the agent's execution flow and session services.
An ADK component that provides the API for dynamic tool injection, allowing tools to be added per invocation.
A google-adk component for managing session state, suitable for single-instance applications.
A context object passed to toolset methods, providing read-only access to agent state.
A custom implementation of BaseToolset designed to provide dynamically discovered skills to the agent.
An abstract base class for defining individual tools within google-adk.
A custom implementation of BaseTool that bridges external skill definitions (skill.json) to ADK tool declarations.
A type from google.genai used by ADK to declare a tool's capabilities, including its name, description, and input schema.
The verified pattern for executing TypeScript skills via Deno in a separate process from Python.
Guidelines and patterns for managing asynchronous operations within the ADK and Python environment.
Instructions for setting up the development environment and installing required dependencies.
A comparison of recommended technologies with other options, highlighting reasons for the current choice.
The core programming language runtime for the project.
The Python library providing the agent framework components (LlmAgent, Runner, BaseToolset, FunctionTool).
A library for data validation, used for schema validation (e.g., skill.json input_schema).
An asynchronous HTTP client for making network requests, e.g., fetching catalog files.
A YAML parser library for handling YAML configuration files like catalog.yaml.
A library for loading environment variables from .env files.
A library for async primitives, providing an abstraction over asyncio and trio.
A library for validating JSON data against a JSON schema.
Python's built-in library for working with JSON data.
A module from google.genai containing data structures like FunctionDeclaration.
An asynchronous method of BaseToolset that returns a list of tools for the agent.
An asynchronous method of BaseToolset for performing cleanup when the toolset is no longer needed.
A protected method of SkillTool that generates the google.genai.types.FunctionDeclaration for the skill.
An asynchronous method of BaseTool (and SkillTool) that executes the tool's logic.
A custom asynchronous function responsible for invoking a TypeScript skill via a Deno subprocess.
A method of an asyncio subprocess object for sending input and reading output/error streams.
A method of an asyncio subprocess object for terminating the process abruptly.
An asynchronous method of an asyncio subprocess object that waits for the process to terminate.
A JSON function for serializing a Python dict to a JSON formatted string.
A JSON function for deserializing a JSON formatted string to a Python dict.
A function from python-dotenv for loading environment variables from a .env file.
A pyyaml function for safely parsing a YAML string into a Python object.
An anyio primitive for running a block of code with a timeout, allowing it to complete or be cancelled.
A configuration setting for pytest-asyncio to automatically manage the asyncio event loop for async tests.
A critical decision to restrict google-genai to major version 1 due to incompatibility with google-adk 1.33.0 and google-genai 2.x.
The choice to use Deno as the runtime for TypeScript skills due to its security features and sandbox capabilities.
The decision to use asyncio's native subprocess creation for Deno invocation, ensuring non-blocking behavior and proper timeout handling.
The choice to use httpx as the async HTTP client, leveraging its existing presence as a transitive dependency.
The decision to exclusively use yaml.safe_load for parsing YAML files to prevent security vulnerabilities.
The API key required for authenticating with the Gemini API.
The core LLM agent, powered by Google's Gemini model, responsible for skill discovery and execution.
Dynamically discoverable and executable units of functionality, hosted in a GitHub catalog.
The isolated execution environment for TypeScript skills, leveraging Deno's deny-by-default permissions.
A data validation library required by ADK for defining models and contracts.
The TypeScript runtime and secure sandbox used for executing skills.
The architectural decision to use `proc.communicate()` instead of `proc.wait()` to prevent subprocess pipe deadlocks.
The architectural decision to ensure complete cleanup of Deno subprocesses, including process groups, on timeout or completion.
The architectural decision to address the issue of too many injected tool schemas exhausting the LLM's context window.
The architectural decision to prevent hitting GitHub API rate limits during catalog fetches.
The architectural decision to manage the confidence threshold against potential shifts from LLM model updates.
The architectural decision to constrain Gemini's tag generation to match known catalog tags.
The architectural decision to rigorously validate network permissions (`--allow-net`) injected into Deno commands to prevent security vulnerabilities.
The architectural approach where the agent first extracts tags and evaluates confidence, then potentially fetches and injects tools for a second pass.
The set of core features and functionalities required for the initial launch of the project.
The module containing shared Pydantic data contracts (CatalogManifest, CatalogSkill, SkillDefinition) used across layers.
